title: Freycinetia distigmata

intro: Freycinetia distigmata, also known as the climbing palm or "wild liana", is a species of flowering plant in the family Lomariopsidaceae, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents found in Freycinetia distigmata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
